message:
fix(web): prevent error/panic message leakage in HTTP responses and fix percent_decode edge cases

RegionOutcome::into_response() previously included internal error messages and
panic details in the HTTP response body, which could leak sensitive information
(stack traces, internal state, file paths) to external clients. Error and
Panicked variants now return a generic "Internal Server Error" body, with the
original detail available only through server-side logging.

percent_decode() rewritten to use index-based iteration instead of consuming
a byte iterator, fixing incorrect behavior on incomplete percent sequences:
- "%2" at end of string now preserves "%2" literally instead of consuming
  the trailing byte and emitting just "%"
- Invalid hex pairs like "%GG" are preserved verbatim instead of being
  partially consumed
- Lone "%" at end of input preserved correctly

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
